content: Just as the lexical definition of "reflexion" includes both the physical reflection of light off a surface and the cognitive act of critical self-review, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to separate two complementary functions: passive "reflection" (mirroring inconsistent solution outputs back to the model, as in Self-Contrast) and active "reflexion" (deliberate self-critique to update the model's dynamic memory). This split of the original dual definition of the term unlocks a new, structured approach to building more robust autonomous LLM agents.
